2603.19563.title: Dual-Domain Representation Alignment: Bridging 2D and 3D Vision via Geometry-Aware Architecture Search
2603.19563.paper_summary: Modern computer vision requires balancing predictive accuracy with real-time efficiency, yet the high inference cost of large vision models (LVMs) limits deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. Although Evolutionary Neural Architecture Search (ENAS) is well suited for multi-objective optimization, its practical use is hindered by two issues: expensive candidate evaluation and ranking inconsistency among subnetworks. To address them, we propose EvoNAS, an efficient distributed framework for multi-objective evolutionary architecture search. We build a hybrid supernet that integrates Vision State Space and Vision Transformer (VSS-ViT) modules, and optimize it with a Cross-Architecture Dual-Domain Knowledge Distillation (CA-DDKD) strategy. By coupling the computational efficiency of VSS blocks with the semantic expressiveness of ViT modules, CA-DDKD improves the representational capacity of the shared supernet and enhances ranking consistency, enabling reliable fitness estimation during evolution without extra fine-tuning. To reduce the cost of large-scale validation, we further introduce a Distributed Multi-Model Parallel Evaluation (DMMPE) framework based on GPU resource pooling and asynchronous scheduling. Compared with conventional data-parallel evaluation, DMMPE improves efficiency by over 70% through concurrent multi-GPU, multi-model execution. Experiments on COCO, ADE20K, KITTI, and NYU-Depth v2 show that the searched architectures, termed EvoNets, consistently achieve Pareto-optimal trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency. Compared with representative CNN-, ViT-, and Mamba-based models, EvoNets deliver lower inference latency and higher throughput under strict computational budgets while maintaining strong generalization on downstream tasks such as novel view synthesis. Code is available at https://github.com/EMI-Group/evonas
2603.19563.paper_summary_zh: 現代計算機視覺需要在預測準確性與實時效率之間取得平衡，但大型視覺模型（LVMs）高昂的推理成本限制了其在資源受限的邊緣設備上的部署。雖然演化神經架構搜索（ENAS）非常適合多目標優化，但其實際應用受到兩個問題的阻礙：候選者評估成本高昂以及子網絡之間的排名不一致。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了EvoNAS，一個高效的分佈式多目標演化架構搜索框架。我們建立了一個混合超網絡，整合了視覺狀態空間和視覺Transformer（VSS-ViT）模塊，並使用跨架構雙域知識蒸餾（CA-DDKD）策略對其進行優化。通過將VSS模塊的計算效率與ViT模塊的語義表達能力相結合，CA-DDKD提高了共享超網絡的表現能力並增強了排名的一致性，使得在演化過程中能夠可靠地估算適應度，而無需額外的微調。為了降低大規模驗證的成本，我們進一步引入了一個基於GPU資源池和異步調度的分佈式多模型並行評估（DMMPE）框架。與傳統的數據並行評估相比，DMMPE通過並行的多GPU、多模型執行提高了超過70%的效率。在COCO、ADE20K、KITTI和NYU-Depth v2上的實驗顯示，所搜索的架構，稱為EvoNets，始終在準確性和效率之間實現了Pareto最優的權衡。與代表性的CNN、ViT和Mamba基礎模型相比，EvoNets在嚴格的計算預算下提供了更低的推理延遲和更高的吞吐量，同時在新穎視圖合成等下游任務上保持強大的泛化能力。代碼可在 https://github.com/EMI-Group/evonas 獲得。
2603.19563.author: Haoyu Zhang et.al.
2603.19563.authors: Haoyu Zhang, Zhihao Yu, Rui Wang, Yaochu Jin, Qiqi Liu, Ran Cheng
